channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: What to know about martial law and the effort to impeach South Korea's president
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — South Korea's President Yoon Suk Yeol is facing possible impeachment for sending heavily armed soldiers into Seoul's streets with a baffling declaration of martial law that reminded many of the country’s past military-backed dictatorships.
item.dc:creator: FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK leader Starmer will outline his 'Plan for Change.' Just don't call it a relaunch
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is trying to change the narrative on his five-month-old government after plummeting approval ratings, business anxiety over tax hikes and protesting farmers clogging London streets.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Yoon replaces the defense minister as South Korea's parliament moves to vote on their impeachments
item.description: South Korea’s president has replaced his defense minister as opposition parties moved to impeach both men over the stunning-but-brief imposition of martial law that brought armed troops into Seoul streets.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim And Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korean president's martial law declaration a 'blunder,' could embolden North Korea, expert says
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law and now faces impeachment over the surprising move from opposition lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Opposition party leader arrested as Georgia authorities try to curb protests over ending EU talks
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Georgian police on Wednesday raided the offices of an opposition party and arrested its leader in an apparent attempt to squelch a wave of mass protests triggered by the governing party’s decision to suspend negotiations on joining the European Union.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Voters want more US involvement on world stage despite isolationist talk, Ronald Reagan Institute survey finds
item.description: The Ronald Reagan Institute released its sixth annual National Defense Survey and found 'record high' support for U.S. involvement abroad despite a growing sense that isolationism could be on the rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Takeaways from Biden's Africa trip: Pardon of son Hunter overshadows official business
item.description: President Joe Biden’s long-delayed trip to Africa was overshadowed by his controversial pardon of his son Hunter.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: French lawmakers vote to oust prime minister in the first successful no-confidence vote since 1962
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France’s far-right and left-wing lawmakers joined together Wednesday in a historic no-confidence vote prompted by budget disputes that forces Prime Minister Michel Barnier and his Cabinet members to resign, a first since 1962. The National Assembly approved the motion by 331 votes.
item.dc:creator: TOM NOUVIAN and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A major power plant fails in Cuba, plunging the island into darkness — again
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — A new, widespread power outage plunged Cuba into darkness on Wednesday after one of the island’s major power plants failed, leaving millions without electricity and forcing authorities to suspend classes and work activities indefinitely.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Romania's surprise front-runner says he's not pro-Russia. But Romania alleges he had Moscow's help
item.description: IZVORANI, Romania (AP) — The surprise front-runner in Romania’s presidential election insisted to The Associated Press in an interview Wednesday that he is not pro-Russian — hours before Romania declassified intelligence alleging Moscow ran an online campaign to promote him to voters.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and VADIM GHIRDA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump picks Monica Crowley for senior State Department post
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday nominated Monica Crowley, a former Fox News contributor who faced public scrutiny during Trump’s first term over allegations of plagiarism, for a top public relations post in the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Johnson rejects Biden request for $24 billion on new Ukraine aid
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson on Wednesday rejected a White House request to pass $24 billion in additional aid for Ukraine by the end of the year, saying any further assistance for Kyiv would be decided by President-elect Trump when he assumes office in January.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: NATO chief urges European allies to ramp up defense spending as Trump returns to White House
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte led a fresh push Wednesday for European countries to ramp up defense spending, a budget shortfall that President-elect Donald Trump used to berate U.S. allies during his first term in office, severely damaging trust.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philippines says China's coast guard fired water cannons and sideswiped its patrol vessel
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Chinese coast guard vessels backed by navy ships fired powerful water cannons and blocked and sideswiped a Philippine patrol vessel on Wednesday in renewed aggression at a disputed shoal in the South China Sea, Philippine officials said.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine ceasefire terms come into focus as Trump returns
item.description: Potential paths to a ceasefire in Ukraine are coming into focus as President-elect Trump approaches Inauguration Day, with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky signaling his concessions to end the war largely along the current front lines in the east.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Power shortages in Ecuador are melting away the future of a small town’s ice-cream industry
item.description: SALCEDO, Ecuador (AP) — Ice-cream production in Salcedo, a quaint town in Ecuador's central highlands, began in the mid-20th century, born from the ingenuity of Franciscan nuns.
item.dc:creator: GONZALO SOLANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Demonstrators again converge on Georgia's parliament after the country's EU bid was suspended
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Thousands of demonstrators in the Georgian capital converged on parliament again on Tuesday, venting outrage against the governing party's decision to suspend negotiations on joining the European Union.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea lifts president's martial law decree after lawmakers reject military rule
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The president of South Korea early Wednesday lifted the martial law he imposed on the country hours earlier, bending to political pressure after a tense night in which troops surrounded parliament and lawmakers voted to reject military rule.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean president lifts martial law after lawmakers reject move
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol reportedly lifted a martial law declaration after lawmakers rejected the move hours after it was announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jill Biden to attend reopening of Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris
item.description: First lady Jill Biden will travel to Paris on Saturday for the reopening of Notre Dame Cathedral, her office announced on Tuesday. She will already be abroad and travel from Doha, Qatar, to Paris that day before returning home to Washington on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: South Korean parliament blocks president's martial law declaration
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol attempted to declare martial law in the country for the first time in 45 years but was blocked by a parliament vote early Wednesday morning local time. He vowed to lift the order after hours of chaos and protests in the capital of Seoul.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: South Korean president declares martial law in move against opposition party
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law on Tuesday and accused the opposition party of taking the parliamentary process hostage with "anti-state" activities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinians say Hamas, Fatah close to agreement on committee for postwar Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Palestinian officials say Fatah and Hamas are closing in on an agreement to appoint a committee of politically independent technocrats to administer the Gaza Strip after the war. It would effectively end Hamas' rule and could help advance ceasefire talks with Israel.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 11 in Lebanon after fire exchanges with Hezbollah that test ceasefire’s limits
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel unleashed its largest wave of airstrikes across Lebanon since agreeing to a ceasefire with Hezbollah last week, killing at least 11 people on Monday after the Lebanese militant group fired a volley of projectiles as a warning over what it said were Israeli truce violations.
item.dc:creator: NATALIE MELTZER and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russian tourist dies after being swept away by massive wave while doing yoga in Thailand
item.description: Russian tourist Kamilla Beliatskaya died on Friday after she was swept away by a large wave while doing yoga on a rock in Koh Samui, Thailand.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden administration to announce $725M weapons package to Ukraine months before Trump is sworn in
item.description: The Biden administration is announcing a $725 million weapons package for Ukraine less than two months before President-elect Donald Trump takes office, a U.S. official says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pro-Putin leader backs harsh crackdown as over 40 people hospitalized amid protests in Georgia
item.description: Dozens are hospitalized after massive protests broke out across Georgia following the pro-Putin government's move halting decadeslong talks on joining the EU amid increasing ties with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Volkswagen workers hold strikes to push back against proposed pay cuts
item.description: Volkswagen workers launched rolling strikes at nine plants across Germany to underscore their resistance to pay cuts
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Customers at this Starbucks can sip coffee and observe a quiet North Korean village
item.description: Coffee drinkers can sip their beverages and view a quiet North Korean mountain village from a new Starbucks at a South Korean border observatory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: British lawmakers give initial approval to a bill to allow terminally ill adults to end their lives
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British lawmakers gave initial approval on Friday to a bill to help terminally ill adults end their lives in England and Wales, following an impassioned debate that saw people sharing personal stories of loss and suffering.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Stowaway who boarded New York-to-Paris flight will be sent back to US
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Authorities are investigating after a Russian national who did not have a ticket boarded a Paris-bound Delta flight at New York's John F. Kennedy International Airport this week and was arrested when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Missing hiker survived 50 days in Canadian wilderness, at times in frigid temps
item.description: A 20-year-old hiker who had been missing for weeks was found alive in the wilderness of British Columbia, the Royal Canadian Mountain Police confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: Millions in Ukraine without power after Russia attacks grid
item.description: “Attacks on energy facilities are happening all over Ukraine,” Energy Minister Herman Halushchenko said on Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Stowaway found on Delta flight from New York City to Paris
item.description: The TSA says the woman bypassed two identity verification areas and boarding status stations before getting on the plane.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Death toll rises to at least 15 after landslides bury 40 homes in eastern Uganda
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — At least 15 people have died and 113 others are missing after landslides buried homes in six villages in eastern Uganda, police said Thursday. Another 15 injured people have been rescued and admitted to Buluganya Health Center.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
